Package com.wip.exception

Class UiExceptionHandler

java.lang.Object
com.wip.exception.UiExceptionHandler
@ControllerAdvice(annotations=org.springframework.stereotype.Controller.class) public class UiExceptionHandler extends Object
Centralised exception handler for Thymeleaf UI controllers annotated with Controller.

This class is annotated with @ControllerAdvice scoped exclusively to @Controller beans. Rather than returning JSON error payloads, it renders proper HTML error pages so that browser-facing users receive user-friendly error information. REST controllers annotated with @RestController are handled separately by GlobalExceptionHandler.

Each handler method populates two standard model attributes before returning a view name:

  • errorTitle — a concise title summarising the nature of the error
  • errorMessage — a descriptive message with actionable information for the user
Since:
1.0
Version:
1.0
Author:
Rohith Varma K

  • Constructor Details

    • UiExceptionHandler

      public UiExceptionHandler()

  • Method Details

    • handleNotFound

      @ExceptionHandler(ResourceNotFoundException.class) public String handleNotFound(ResourceNotFoundException ex, org.springframework.ui.Model model)
      Handles ResourceNotFoundException thrown by UI controllers when a requested record cannot be found in the system.

      Adds the exception message as the errorMessage model attribute and renders the error/not-found Thymeleaf template with an HTTP 200 response (error status is communicated visually to the user rather than via HTTP headers).

      Parameters:
      ex - the ResourceNotFoundException carrying the "not found" detail message
      model - the Spring MVC Model used to pass error details to the view; populated with errorTitle and errorMessage
      Returns:
      the logical view name "error/not-found"

    • handleBadRequest

      @ExceptionHandler(java.lang.IllegalStateException.class) public String handleBadRequest(IllegalStateException ex, org.springframework.ui.Model model)
      Handles IllegalStateException thrown by UI controllers when a requested action violates a business rule or is otherwise not permitted.

      Common scenarios include attempting an operation on an entity that is in an incompatible state. Renders the error/bad-request Thymeleaf template.

      Parameters:
      ex - the IllegalStateException describing the disallowed action
      model - the Spring MVC Model used to pass error details to the view; populated with errorTitle and errorMessage
      Returns:
      the logical view name "error/bad-request"

    • handleAccessDenied

      @ExceptionHandler(org.springframework.security.access.AccessDeniedException.class) public String handleAccessDenied(Exception ex, org.springframework.ui.Model model)
      Handles AccessDeniedException thrown when the authenticated user attempts to access a resource or perform an action they are not authorised to perform.

      Renders the error/access-denied Thymeleaf template with a generic permission-denied message so that sensitive internal details are not disclosed.

      Parameters:
      ex - the Exception representing the access-denial condition (typed as Exception to satisfy the Spring MVC handler signature)
      model - the Spring MVC Model used to pass error details to the view; populated with errorTitle and errorMessage
      Returns:
      the logical view name "error/access-denied"

    • handleGeneral

      @ExceptionHandler(java.lang.Exception.class) public String handleGeneral(Exception ex, org.springframework.ui.Model model)
      Handles any unrecognised Exception not matched by a more specific handler within the UI controller layer.

      Acts as a catch-all to prevent raw stack traces from being displayed to the user. The exception is printed to standard error for server-side diagnosis. The error/general Thymeleaf template is rendered with a safe, generic message that advises the user to retry or contact support.

      Parameters:
      ex - the unexpected Exception that was not matched by a more specific handler
      model - the Spring MVC Model used to pass error details to the view; populated with errorTitle and errorMessage
      Returns:
      the logical view name "error/general"
